### Example Python Script for Web Scraping

```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import pandas as pd

def scrape_website(url):
    response = requests.get(url)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')
    
    # Define tags and attributes to extract data
    data = []
    for tag in soup.find_all('div', class_='chemical-data'):
        name = tag.find('h2').text.strip()
        quantity = tag.find('span', class_='quantity').text.strip()
        data.append({'Chemical': name, 'Quantity': quantity})
    
    return data

url = 'https://example.com/chemicals'
data = scrape_website(url)

# Convert to DataFrame
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
print(df)

# Save to CSV
df.to_csv('chemical_data.csv', index=False)
```

### Example Python Script for PubMed API

```python
import requests

def get_pubmed_data(query):
    api_key = 'your_api_key'
    base_url = 'https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/esearch.fcgi'
    params = {
        'db': 'pubmed',
        'term': query,
        'api_key': api_key
    }
    response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
    result = response.json()
    ids = result['esearchresult']['idlist']
    return ids

query = 'industrial pollution'
pmids = get_pubmed_data(query)

# Fetch full text using PMIDs
base_url = 'https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/efetch.fcgi'
params = {
    'db': 'pubmed',
    'id': ','.join(pmids),
    'retmode': 'xml',
    'api_key': api_key
}
response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
xml_data = response.content

# Parse XML and extract relevant data
from xml.etree import ElementTree as ET
root = ET.fromstring(xml_data)
articles = []
for article in root.findall('.//PubmedArticle'):
    title = article.find('.//ArticleTitle').text
    abstract = article.find('.//AbstractText').text
    articles.append({'Title': title, 'Abstract': abstract})

df = pd.DataFrame(articles)
print(df)

# Save to CSV
df.to_csv('pubmed_data.csv', index=False)
```

```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import pandas as pd

def scrape_website(url):
    response = requests.get(url)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')
    
    # Define tags and attributes to extract data
    data = []
    for row in soup.find_all('tr')[1:]:  # Skip header row
        columns = row.find_all('td')
        if len(columns) > 0:
            chemical_name = columns[0].text.strip()
            industry = columns[1].text.strip()
            quantity = columns[2].text.strip()
            impact = columns[3].text.strip()
            data.append({
                'Chemical': chemical_name,
                'Industry': industry,
                'Quantity': quantity,
                'Impact': impact
            })
    
    return data

url = 'https://example.com/harmful-chemicals'
data = scrape_website(url)

# Convert to DataFrame
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
print(df)

# Save to CSV
df.to_csv('scraped_data.csv', index=False)
```

#### 2. **PubMed API Script**
Next, we'll use the PubMed API to find relevant academic papers and extract additional data.

```python
import requests

def get_pubmed_data(query, api_key):
    base_url = 'https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/esearch.fcgi'
    params = {
        'db': 'pubmed',
        'term': query,
        'api_key': api_key
    }
    response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
    result = response.json()
    ids = result['esearchresult']['idlist']
    return ids

def fetch_pmc_xml(ids, api_key):
    base_url = 'https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/efetch.fcgi'
    pmc_ids = [f'PMC{pmid}' for pmid in ids]
    params = {
        'db': 'pmc',
        'id': ','.join(pmc_ids),
        'retmode': 'xml',
        'api_key': api_key
    }
    response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
    return response.content

def parse_pmc_xml(xml_data):
    from xml.etree import ElementTree as ET
    root = ET.fromstring(xml_data)
    articles = []
    for article in root.findall('.//article'):
        title = article.find('.//title').text
        abstract = article.find('.//abstract').text
        articles.append({'Title': title, 'Abstract': abstract})
    
    return articles

# Example usage
query = 'industrial pollution'
api_key = 'your_api_key_here'
pmids = get_pubmed_data(query, api_key)
xml_data = fetch_pmc_xml(pmids, api_key)
articles = parse_pmc_xml(xml_data)

# Convert to DataFrame
df_articles = pd.DataFrame(articles)
print(df_articles)

# Save to CSV
df_articles.to_csv('pubmed_data.csv', index=False)
```

### Combining Both Scripts
To combine both scripts and ensure the data is comprehensive, we can run the web scraping and API queries sequentially and merge the results.

```python
import requests

    response = requests.get(url)

        columns = row.find_all('td')

            data.append({
                'Chemical': chemical_name,
                'Industry': industry,
                'Quantity': quantity,
                'Impact': impact
            })
    
    return data

# PubMed API Function
def get_pubmed_data(query, api_key):
    base_url = 'https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/esearch.fcgi'
    params = {
        'db': 'pubmed',
        'term': query,
        'api_key': api_key
    }
    response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
    result = response.json()
    ids = result['esearchresult']['idlist']
    return ids

def fetch_pmc_xml(ids, api_key):
    base_url = 'https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/efetch.fcgi'
    pmc_ids = [f'PMC{pmid}' for pmid in ids]
    params = {
        'db': 'pmc',
        'id': ','.join(pmc_ids),
        'retmode': 'xml',
        'api_key': api_key
    }
    response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
    return response.content

def parse_pmc_xml(xml_data):
    root = ET.fromstring(xml_data)
    articles = []
    for article in root.findall('.//article'):
        title = article.find('.//title').text
        abstract = article.find('.//abstract').text
        articles.append({'Title': title, 'Abstract': abstract})
    
    return articles

# Main Script

    url = 'https://example.com/harmful-chemicals'

```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import pandas as pd

def scrape_website(url):
    response = requests.get(url)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')
    
    # Define tags and attributes to extract data
    data = []
    for row in soup.find_all('tr')[1:]:  # Skip header row
        columns = row.find_all('td')
        if len(columns) > 0:
            chemical_name = columns[0].text.strip()
            industry = columns[1].text.strip()
            quantity = columns[2].text.strip()
            impact = columns[3].text.strip()
            data.append({
                'Chemical': chemical_name,
                'Industry': industry,
                'Quantity': quantity,
                'Impact': impact
            })
    
    return data

url = 'https://example.com/harmful-chemicals'
data = scrape_website(url)

# Convert to DataFrame
df = pd.DataFrame(data)
print(df)

# Save to CSV
df.to_csv('scraped_data.csv', index=False)
```

#### 2. **PubMed API Script**
Next, we'll use the PubMed API to find relevant academic papers and extract additional data.

```python
import requests

def get_pubmed_data(query, api_key):
    base_url = 'https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/esearch.fcgi'
    params = {
        'db': 'pubmed',
        'term': query,
        'api_key': api_key
    }
    response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
    result = response.json()
    ids = result['esearchresult']['idlist']
    return ids

def fetch_pmc_xml(ids, api_key):
    base_url = 'https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/efetch.fcgi'
    pmc_ids = [f'PMC{pmid}' for pmid in ids]
    params = {
        'db': 'pmc',
        'id': ','.join(pmc_ids),
        'retmode': 'xml',
        'api_key': api_key
    }
    response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
    return response.content

def parse_pmc_xml(xml_data):
    from xml.etree import ElementTree as ET
    root = ET.fromstring(xml_data)
    articles = []
    for article in root.findall('.//article'):
        title = article.find('.//title').text
        abstract = article.find('.//abstract').text
        articles.append({'Title': title, 'Abstract': abstract})
    
    return articles

# Example usage
query = 'industrial pollution'
api_key = 'your_api_key_here'
pmids = get_pubmed_data(query, api_key)
xml_data = fetch_pmc_xml(pmids, api_key)
articles = parse_pmc_xml(xml_data)

# Convert to DataFrame
df_articles = pd.DataFrame(articles)
print(df_articles)

# Save to CSV
df_articles.to_csv('pubmed_data.csv', index=False)
```

import requests

def scrape_website(url):
    response = requests.get(url)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')
    
    data = []
    for row in soup.find_all('tr')[1:]:  # Skip header row
        columns = row.find_all('td')
        if len(columns) > 0:
            chemical_name = columns[0].text.strip()
            industry = columns[1].text.strip()
            quantity = columns[2].text.strip()
            impact = columns[3].text.strip()
            data.append({
                'Chemical': chemical_name,
                'Industry': industry,
                'Quantity': quantity,
                'Impact': impact
            })
    
    return data

# PubMed API Function
def get_pubmed_data(query, api_key):
    base_url = 'https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/esearch.fcgi'
    params = {
        'db': 'pubmed',
        'term': query,
        'api_key': api_key
    }
    response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
    result = response.json()
    ids = result['esearchresult']['idlist']
    return ids

def fetch_pmc_xml(ids, api_key):
    base_url = 'https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/efetch.fcgi'
    pmc_ids = [f'PMC{pmid}' for pmid in ids]
    params = {
        'db': 'pmc',
        'id': ','.join(pmc_ids),
        'retmode': 'xml',
        'api_key': api_key
    }
    response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
    return response.content

def parse_pmc_xml(xml_data):
    root = ET.fromstring(xml_data)
    articles = []
    for article in root.findall('.//article'):
        title = article.find('.//title').text
        abstract = article.find('.//abstract').text
        articles.append({'Title': title, 'Abstract': abstract})
    
    return articles

# Main Script
if __name__ == "__main__":
    url = 'https://example.com/harmful-chemicals'
    data = scrape_website(url)
    df_scraped = pd.DataFrame(data)
    print("Scraped Data:")

    print("PubMed Data:")

    print("Merged Data:")
    print(merged_df)
    merged_df.to_csv('merged_data.csv', index=False)

```python
import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup
import pandas as pd

def scrape_website(url):
    response = requests.get(url)
    soup = BeautifulSoup(response.text, 'html.parser')
    
    # Define tags and attributes to extract data
    data = []
    for row in soup.find_all('tr')[1:]:  # Skip header row
        columns = row.find_all('td')
        if len(columns) > 0:
            chemical_name = columns[0].text.strip()
            industry = columns[1].text.strip()
            quantity = columns[2].text.strip()
            impact = columns[3].text.strip()
            data.append({
                'Chemical': chemical_name,
                'Industry': industry,
                'Quantity': quantity,
                'Impact': impact
            })
    
    return data

url = 'https://example.com/harmful-chemicals'
data = scrape_website(url)

# Convert to DataFrame
df_scraped = pd.DataFrame(data)
print(df_scraped)

# Save to CSV
df_scraped.to_csv('scraped_data.csv', index=False)
```

#### 2. **PubMed API Script**
Next, we'll use the PubMed API to find relevant academic papers and extract additional data.

```python
import requests

def get_pubmed_data(query, api_key):
    base_url = 'https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/esearch.fcgi'
    params = {
        'db': 'pubmed',
        'term': query,
        'api_key': api_key
    }
    response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
    result = response.json()
    ids = result['esearchresult']['idlist']
    return ids

def fetch_pmc_xml(ids, api_key):
    base_url = 'https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/efetch.fcgi'
    pmc_ids = [f'PMC{pmid}' for pmid in ids]
    params = {
        'db': 'pmc',
        'id': ','.join(pmc_ids),
        'retmode': 'xml',
        'api_key': api_key
    }
    response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
    return response.content

def parse_pmc_xml(xml_data):
    from xml.etree import ElementTree as ET
    root = ET.fromstring(xml_data)
    articles = []
    for article in root.findall('.//article'):
        title = article.find('.//title').text
        abstract = article.find('.//abstract').text
        articles.append({'Title': title, 'Abstract': abstract})
    
    return articles

# Example usage

xml_data = fetch_pmc_xml(pmids, api_key)
articles = parse_pmc_xml(xml_data)

# Convert to DataFrame
df_articles = pd.DataFrame(articles)
print(df_articles)

# Save to CSV
df_articles.to_csv('pubmed_data.csv', index=False)
```

    params = {
        'db': 'pubmed',

        'api_key': api_key
    }
    response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
    result = response.json()
    ids = result['esearchresult']['idlist']
    return ids

def fetch_pmc_xml(ids, api_key):
    base_url = 'https://eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/entrez/eutils/efetch.fcgi'
    pmc_ids = [f'PMC{pmid}' for pmid in ids]
    params = {
        'db': 'pmc',
        'id': ','.join(pmc_ids),
        'retmode': 'xml',
        'api_key': api_key
    }
    response = requests.get(base_url, params=params)
    return response.content

def parse_pmc_xml(xml_data):
    root = ET.fromstring(xml_data)
    articles = []
    for article in root.findall('.//article'):
        title = article.find('.//title').text
        abstract = article.find('.//abstract').text
        articles.append({'Title': title, 'Abstract': abstract})
    
    return articles

# Main Script
